skill.name: child-agent-ops
skill.description: Standardizes child agent startup, task handoff, reporting, review, and safe operation for Codex-based work.
skill.summary: Coordinate child agents with clear roles, logs, checkpoints, reviews, and handoff templates.
skill.author: A-Genno

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a weak cryptography pattern, and high entropy. Manual review found no executable Ruby or shell code, no cryptographic implementation, no encoded payload, and no prompt injection text in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is low because the skill instructs users to operate Codex child agents and maintain logs, but it does not run commands itself.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown inline code or fenced template content describing the allowed Codex command and reporting formats. No evidence found of executable Ruby, shell interpolation, or command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and each reported backtick is part of inline command naming or a Markdown report template. There is no runtime file or interpreter context that would execute these strings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported location is front matter description text for the skill. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, signing, password storage, or weak cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of SKILL.md shows line 3 is natural-language metadata. The file contains no crypto API calls or algorithm names used as implementation.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The file contains Japanese Markdown text, which can trigger entropy heuristics. No evidence found of binary content, encrypted data, packed code, or opaque encoded blobs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is readable Markdown with front matter and operational guidance. The entropy signal appears caused by normal UTF-8 Japanese content, not obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Teams using AI agents can lose context when tasks split across many workers. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear operating model for delegation, reporting, review, and safe closure.
actual_capabilities: Defines leader, implementation, and review agent responsibilities.
Explains when child agents should or should not be launched.
Provides a standard workflow for task division, checkpoints, completion reports, and shutdown.
Includes reporting templates for task status and child agent startup logs.
Lists safety checks for high-risk changes, external access, billing, and data deletion.
Recommends review agent use when code is changed or created.
limitations: Does not include executable automation for launching or stopping agents.
Does not integrate with a task tracker, queue, or observability system.
Does not define enforcement controls for permissions or command approval.
Uses Japanese source content, so teams may need localized operating notes.

use_cases.title: Split implementation work across agents
use_cases.description: Use clear roles, completion criteria, and checkpoints before delegating work to child agents.

use_cases.title: Standardize investigation handoffs
use_cases.description: Give research agents a consistent way to report findings, risks, blockers, and next actions.

use_cases.title: Add review gates to agent workflows
use_cases.description: Start a review agent after code changes and record review results in the task log.

prompt_templates.title: Start a simple child agent task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use child-agent-ops to prepare a child agent launch note for this task: [task]. Include role, scope, completion criteria, expected time, and reporting method.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a multi-agent workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use child-agent-ops to split this goal into leader, implementation, investigation, and review responsibilities: [goal]. Include checkpoints and escalation triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Create a progress report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use child-agent-ops to write a concise progress report for these agent updates: [updates]. Highlight completed work, blockers, risks, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an agent operating plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use child-agent-ops to review this child agent plan for unclear scope, missing approvals, unsafe parallel updates, review gaps, and shutdown steps: [plan].

output_examples.input: Launch a child agent to investigate a failing test suite.
output_examples.output: A short launch note that names the investigation role, limits the scope to test failure analysis, defines completion as a root cause summary, and requests a checkpoint.

output_examples.input: Several agents report blockers during a refactor.
output_examples.output: A consolidated status report with completed work, unresolved blockers, affected files or logs, risk notes, and a proposed next action for the leader.

output_examples.input: A code-writing child agent finished its task.
output_examples.output: A completion report that records the changed area, observed risks, follow-up actions, and the need to start a review agent.

best_practices: Define scope, completion criteria, and expected duration before launching a child agent.
Escalate unclear specifications, security risks, billing impact, and data deletion before continuing.
Start a review agent whenever code is changed or created.
anti_patterns: Launching child agents for tightly coupled tasks that cannot be separated.
Letting multiple agents modify the same area without leader coordination.
Closing a child agent task without recording outputs, risks, and next actions.

faq.question: Does this skill launch agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides operating guidance, checklists, and templates for human or AI-led coordination.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use a child agent?
faq.answer: Use one when the task is independent, benefits from parallel work, or needs specialized investigation.

faq.question: When should I avoid a child agent?
faq.answer: Avoid one when dependencies are too tight or coordination cost is higher than the expected benefit.

faq.question: Does it include safety guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It calls for leader confirmation on unclear specifications, compatibility risk, security, billing, and data deletion.

faq.question: Does it require code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs teams to start a review agent after code changes or new code creation.
